---
title: "`r params$title`"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
params:
  title: "RNAdetector Small RNA-Seq Analysis Report"
  pipeline_version: "2.0"
  analysis_type: "small_rna"
  algorithm: "star"
  counting_algorithm: "featurecounts"
  de_algorithm: "deseq2"
  genome_name: "Human (hg38)"
  annotation_name: "GENCODE v38"
  threads: 4
  memory_gb: 8
  trim_enabled: true
  trim_quality: 20
  trim_length: 18
  output_dir: "."
  job_name: "small_rna_analysis"
  input_files: ""
  pathway_analysis: false
  circ_rna_detection: false
  circ_algorithm: "ciri2"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 4
    theme: darkly
    highlight: monokai
    code_folding: show
    self_contained: true
    number_sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
csl: nature.csl
---

```{css custom-dark-theme, echo=TRUE}
/* Custom CSS for dark theme report styling */
body {
  font-family: 'Segoe UI', Tahoma, Geneva, Verdana, sans-serif;
  line-height: 1.7;
}
body p {
  text-align: justify;
}
.table-striped > tbody > tr:nth-of-type(odd) {
  background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.05);
}
pre {
  border: 1px solid #444;
}
```

```{r setup, echo=TRUE}
# Load required libraries for small RNA-Seq analysis reporting
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(knitr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(DT)
})

# Set global chunk options: all code visible
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.align = "center"
)

# Set ggplot2 theme for dark background reports
theme_dark_report <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#222222", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#2d2d2d", color = NA),
    text = element_text(color = "#e0e0e0"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "#cccccc"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#444444"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "#333333"),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "#222222"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "#e0e0e0"),
    plot.title = element_text(color = "#ffffff", size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "#bbbbbb")
  )
```

# Analysis Overview

```{r analysis-overview, echo=TRUE}
# Display summary of the small RNA-Seq analysis parameters
cat("**Job Name:**", params$job_name, "\n\n")
cat("**Analysis Type:** Small RNA-Seq\n\n")
cat("**Reference Genome:**", params$genome_name, "\n\n")
cat("**Annotation:**", params$annotation_name, "\n\n")
cat("**Alignment Algorithm:**", params$algorithm, "\n\n")
cat("**Counting Algorithm:**", params$counting_algorithm, "\n\n")
cat("**DE Algorithm:**", ifelse(is.null(params$de_algorithm), "none", params$de_algorithm), "\n\n")
cat("**Pipeline Version:**", params$pipeline_version, "\n\n")
```

# Materials and Methods

```{r materials-methods, child='materials_methods.Rmd', echo=TRUE}
# Include the shared Materials and Methods child document.
# This generates publication-ready methods text based on the analysis parameters.
```

# Read Size Distribution

```{r load-size-distribution, echo=TRUE}
# Load read length distribution data from the pipeline output
size_dist_file <- file.path(params$output_dir, "read_length_distribution.tsv")
size_dist_file_alt <- file.path(params$output_dir, "size_distribution.tsv")

size_data <- NULL
for (candidate in c(size_dist_file, size_dist_file_alt)) {
  if (file.exists(candidate)) {
    size_data <- read.delim(candidate, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    cat("Read size distribution data loaded.\n")
    break
  }
}

if (is.null(size_data)) {
  cat("Read size distribution file not found in output directory.\n")
  cat("Expected location:", size_dist_file, "\n")
}
```

```{r size-distribution-plot, echo=TRUE, fig.cap="Read length distribution after trimming. Small RNA species are characterized by distinct size ranges: miRNAs (~21-23 nt), piRNAs (~26-31 nt), and tRFs (~14-36 nt)."}
# Plot read length distribution with annotations for expected small RNA sizes
if (!is.null(size_data)) {
  length_col <- grep("length|size|Length|Size", colnames(size_data), value = TRUE)[1]
  count_col <- grep("count|reads|Count|Reads|frequency", colnames(size_data), value = TRUE)[1]

  if (!is.na(length_col) && !is.na(count_col)) {
    plot_df <- data.frame(
      Length = size_data[[length_col]],
      Count = size_data[[count_col]]
    )

    ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Length, y = Count)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#ce93d8", width = 0.8) +
      # Annotate expected small RNA size ranges
      annotate("rect", xmin = 20.5, xmax = 23.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
               alpha = 0.1, fill = "#4fc3f7") +
      annotate("text", x = 22, y = max(plot_df$Count) * 0.95,
               label = "miRNA\n(21-23 nt)", color = "#4fc3f7", size = 3) +
      annotate("rect", xmin = 25.5, xmax = 31.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
               alpha = 0.1, fill = "#81c784") +
      annotate("text", x = 28.5, y = max(plot_df$Count) * 0.85,
               label = "piRNA\n(26-31 nt)", color = "#81c784", size = 3) +
      labs(
        title = "Read Length Distribution",
        subtitle = "After adapter trimming and quality filtering",
        x = "Read Length (nt)",
        y = "Number of Reads"
      ) +
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(10, 50, 2)) +
      theme_dark_report
  }
} else {
  cat("Generating placeholder read size distribution.\n")
  cat("Small RNA size ranges of interest:\n")
  cat("- miRNAs: 21-23 nt\n")
  cat("- piRNAs: 26-31 nt\n")
  cat("- snoRNAs: 60-300 nt\n")
  cat("- tRFs (tRNA-derived fragments): 14-36 nt\n")
}
```

# Small RNA Quantification

```{r load-counts, echo=TRUE}
# Load the small RNA count matrix from the pipeline output
counts_file <- file.path(params$output_dir, "counts_matrix.tsv")
counts_file_alt <- file.path(params$output_dir, "counts.tsv")

count_data <- NULL
for (candidate in c(counts_file, counts_file_alt)) {
  if (file.exists(candidate)) {
    count_data <- read.delim(candidate, header = TRUE, row.names = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    cat("Count matrix loaded:", nrow(count_data), "features x", ncol(count_data), "samples\n")
    break
  }
}

if (is.null(count_data)) {
  cat("Count matrix file not found in output directory.\n")
  cat("Expected location:", counts_file, "\n")
}
```

## RNA Biotype Summary

```{r biotype-summary, echo=TRUE, fig.cap="Distribution of small RNA biotypes detected in the analysis. Each biotype represents a distinct class of non-coding RNA."}
# Summarize detected RNA biotypes if annotation information is available
biotype_file <- file.path(params$output_dir, "biotype_counts.tsv")
biotype_file_alt <- file.path(params$output_dir, "biotype_summary.tsv")

biotype_data <- NULL
for (candidate in c(biotype_file, biotype_file_alt)) {
  if (file.exists(candidate)) {
    biotype_data <- read.delim(candidate, header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    break
  }
}

if (!is.null(biotype_data)) {
  biotype_col <- grep("biotype|type|Biotype|Type", colnames(biotype_data), value = TRUE)[1]
  count_col <- grep("count|reads|Count|Reads|total", colnames(biotype_data), value = TRUE)[1]

  if (!is.na(biotype_col) && !is.na(count_col)) {
    plot_df <- data.frame(
      Biotype = biotype_data[[biotype_col]],
      Count = biotype_data[[count_col]]
    )

    ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = reorder(Biotype, Count), y = Count)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#ffb74d", width = 0.7) +
      coord_flip() +
      labs(
        title = "Small RNA Biotype Distribution",
        x = "RNA Biotype",
        y = "Total Counts"
      ) +
      theme_dark_report
  }
} else {
  cat("Biotype summary file not found. Biotype classification requires annotation data.\n")
  cat("Expected small RNA biotypes include:\n")
  cat("- miRNA (microRNA)\n")
  cat("- piRNA (PIWI-interacting RNA)\n")
  cat("- snoRNA (small nucleolar RNA)\n")
  cat("- snRNA (small nuclear RNA)\n")
  cat("- tRF (tRNA-derived fragment)\n")
  cat("- rRNA (ribosomal RNA)\n")
}
```

## miRNA Expression

```{r mirna-expression, echo=TRUE}
# Extract and display miRNA-specific expression data if available
mirna_file <- file.path(params$output_dir, "mirna_counts.tsv")

if (file.exists(mirna_file)) {
  mirna_data <- read.delim(mirna_file, header = TRUE, row.names = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  cat("miRNA count data loaded:", nrow(mirna_data), "miRNAs detected\n\n")

  # Top miRNAs by mean expression
  mirna_means <- data.frame(
    miRNA = rownames(mirna_data),
    Mean_Count = rowMeans(mirna_data),
    Detected_In = rowSums(mirna_data > 0)
  )
  mirna_means <- mirna_means[order(-mirna_means$Mean_Count), ]
  mirna_means$Mean_Count <- round(mirna_means$Mean_Count, 2)

  datatable(
    head(mirna_means, 30),
    caption = "Top 30 Most Abundant miRNAs",
    options = list(pageLength = 15, dom = "ftip"),
    rownames = FALSE
  )
} else if (!is.null(count_data)) {
  # Try to identify miRNAs from the general count matrix by name pattern
  mirna_rows <- grep("^hsa-mir|^hsa-let|^mmu-mir|^MIR", rownames(count_data), ignore.case = TRUE)

  if (length(mirna_rows) > 0) {
    mirna_subset <- count_data[mirna_rows, , drop = FALSE]
    cat("Identified", nrow(mirna_subset), "miRNAs from the count matrix.\n\n")

    mirna_means <- data.frame(
      miRNA = rownames(mirna_subset),
      Mean_Count = round(rowMeans(mirna_subset), 2),
      Detected_In = rowSums(mirna_subset > 0)
    )
    mirna_means <- mirna_means[order(-mirna_means$Mean_Count), ]

    datatable(
      head(mirna_means, 30),
      caption = "Top 30 Most Abundant miRNAs",
      options = list(pageLength = 15, dom = "ftip"),
      rownames = FALSE
    )
  } else {
    cat("No miRNA-specific data found. miRNA counts may be included in the general count matrix.\n")
  }
}
```

## Expression Distribution

```{r expression-distribution, echo=TRUE, fig.cap="Distribution of log2(counts + 1) across all samples for detected small RNAs."}
# Plot expression distribution for small RNA features
if (!is.null(count_data)) {
  log_counts <- log2(count_data + 1)
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    Expression = unlist(log_counts),
    Sample = rep(colnames(log_counts), each = nrow(log_counts))
  )

  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Expression, color = Sample)) +
    geom_density(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
      title = "Small RNA Expression Distribution",
      subtitle = "Log2(counts + 1) per sample",
      x = "Log2(counts + 1)",
      y = "Density"
    ) +
    theme_dark_report
}
```

## Detection Summary

```{r detection-summary, echo=TRUE}
# Provide a summary of small RNA feature detection across samples
if (!is.null(count_data)) {
  total_features <- nrow(count_data)
  detected_features <- sum(rowSums(count_data) > 0)
  detected_all <- sum(rowSums(count_data > 0) == ncol(count_data))

  cat("**Total features in annotation:**", total_features, "\n\n")
  cat("**Features detected (>0 counts in any sample):**", detected_features,
      sprintf("(%.1f%%)", 100 * detected_features / total_features), "\n\n")
  cat("**Features detected in all samples:**", detected_all,
      sprintf("(%.1f%%)", 100 * detected_all / total_features), "\n\n")
}
```

# Software Versions

```{r software-versions, echo=TRUE}
# Compile a table of all software tools and their versions
software_df <- data.frame(
  Software = c("RNAdetector", "R", "Bioconductor"),
  Version = c(
    params$pipeline_version,
    paste(R.version$major, R.version$minor, sep = "."),
    as.character(BiocManager::version())
  ),
  Reference = c("Alaimo et al., 2020", "R Core Team, 2023", "Huber et al., 2015"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

if (params$algorithm == "star") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "STAR", Version = "2.7.9a", Reference = "Dobin et al., 2013"))
} else if (params$algorithm == "hisat2") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "HISAT2", Version = "2.2.1", Reference = "Kim et al., 2019"))
}

if (params$counting_algorithm == "featurecounts") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "featureCounts", Version = "2.0.3", Reference = "Liao et al., 2014"))
} else if (params$counting_algorithm == "htseq") {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "HTSeq", Version = "0.13.5", Reference = "Anders et al., 2015"))
}

if (params$trim_enabled) {
  software_df <- rbind(software_df, data.frame(Software = "Trim Galore", Version = "0.6.6", Reference = "Krueger, 2015"))
}

kable(software_df, format = "html", caption = "Software Versions") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

# Session Information

```{r session-info, echo=TRUE}
# Print full R session information for complete reproducibility
sessionInfo()
```

# References
